subject: Cloud Computing
title: Cloudflare: The Edge Disruptor
description: A fast breakdown of Cloudflare: The Edge Disruptor for builders.

hook.text: Cloudflare started as a security company. Now it's building a cloud platform at the edge of the internet.

buildup.text: Cloudflare Workers let developers run code on 300+ global edge locations — closer to users than any data center.

discovery.text: Its R2 storage service charges zero egress fees — directly attacking AWS S3's most hated pricing.

twist.text: Cloudflare doesn't have traditional data centers. Its model challenges the entire centralized cloud concept.

climax.text: Cloudflare is betting that the future of cloud is distributed, not centralized. It might be right.

punchline.text: Cloudflare is reimagining cloud from the edge inward.

quiz.question: How does Cloudflare's approach differ from traditional cloud providers?
options: It distributes computing across 300+ edge locations instead of centralized data centers
Cloudflare only provides email hosting services
It requires customers to build their own data centers
